theme: Release water down a reservoir chain in time
title: Cascade Reservoir Chain: Scheduling Irreversible Releases
seed_problem: chain of reservoirs with capacities, a deterministic inflow schedule, downstream travel lags, and demand nodes with flood/drought penalties
mutation: composite: releases are irreversible (forward-only storage bookkeeping, no recall), water travels downstream with a per-hop lag before it counts as arrival, and the full inflow+demand schedule is known in advance so the checker's automatic spill/deficit order (arrival -> demand draw -> forced spill, all BEFORE the period's release choice) makes same-period reaction structurally incapable of preventing a spike overflow -- only a released headroom created in an earlier period can.
innovation_hook: Because releases cannot be recalled and travel with lag, you must pre-empt future inflow spikes and deficits rather than react period by period.
strategies: trivial: release nothing, ever (reproduces the checker's do-nothing baseline exactly)

reactive greedy: release to satisfy the CURRENT period's reported downstream demand (ignoring the lag it will actually take to arrive) plus dump any storage above a fixed high-fill threshold -- reacts to what is visible right now, never anticipates

insight (strong): recursively shift all downstream local demand backward through the lag chain into a per-reservoir per-period release TARGET (a dual/pull-through reformulation independent of physical simulation), then additionally release ahead of any forecast future arrival that would otherwise overflow capacity within a lookahead window -- pre-fills before droughts and pre-empties before spikes
a general solver could go further with a rolling-horizon LP/DP over the full K x T grid instead of the closed-form backward-shift + fixed window
